title: Ma's green bean bredie and wheat
excerpt: Green bean bredie is love food. It's about giving something of yourself to your guests and family. It's noble food that satisfies deeply. I made it for our Saturday evening with wheat and pumpkin. INGREDIENTS; 1.5 kg mutton or beef ( make sure there is a bone in it – extra flavour) …

seo.title: Ma's green bean bredie and wheat - Marinda Kook

html:
Green bean bredie is love food. It's about giving something of yourself to your guests and family. It's noble food that satisfies deeply. I made it for our Saturday evening with wheat and pumpkin.

INGREDIENTS;

  • 1.5 kg mutton or beef ( make sure there is a bone in it – extra flavour)
  • 30 ml butter
  • 30 ml oil
  • 2 onions chopped
  • 3 cloves garlic chopped
  • 1 tin tomatoes or 2 fresh tomatoes finely chopped
  • 1 zest of a lemon plus 125 ml lemon juice
  • 125 ml Worcester sauce
  • 4 potatoes cut into cubes
  • 4 cloves
  • 5 ml ground nutmeg
  • salt and pepper to taste
  • 4 cups or more sliced green beans ( I put in as many as will fit in the pot)
  • 250 ml white wine
  • 250 ml water
  • 20 ml mazina or bistro

NOW:

  1. slice the onions into rings and fry until glassy in a pot in your butter and oil. Add the garlic and fry a little longer.
  2. cut your meat into cubes and fry until lightly browned together with the onions and garlic
  3. stir in your tin of tomatoes
  4. now add the rest of the ingredients except the potatoes, green beans and mazina/bistro and cook for an hour with the lid on. Remember to grate the lemon zest in too on the fine side of the grater.
  5. peel your potatoes and add them to the meat in your pot – cook for another 30 minutes
  6. chop your green beans roughly and add them to the meat mixture too and cook for another 30 minutes
  7. mix the mazina/bistro in a cup with a little water and stir it into your bredie – this is just to thicken your gravy
  8. Now you must start tasting how it's going. Add seasoning now to your taste.

You have to keep an eye on the bredie throughout. I like a lot of gravy with my bredie. Top up with water if it isn't saucy enough for you. The gravy over rice or wheat is a great treat.

WHEAT: much too easy

  1. rinse the wheat under running water
  2. put it in a pot and cover it with water
  3. let it simmer gently with the lid tilted – otherwise it will boil over. It cooks for about 40 minutes.
  4. drain it in a sieve
  5. I only stirred butter through my wheat, but here you have a free hand. Stir in whatever you feel like: raisins, parsley, nuts, chillies, fruit, vegetables etc.

The wheat can be eaten cold or warm. What's left over I usually make into a salad for the next day.
